title: GDPR Storage Limitation Automation Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: GDPR Art.25(2) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.1.49' running on index=backup, aws:cloudtrail (DeleteObject lifecycle), application deletion job logs (HEC), gdpr_retention_schedule.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Veeam App for Splunk (Splunkbase 7312)
spl:
| inputlookup gdpr_retention_schedule.csv
| join dataset_name [
    search index=app sourcetype="deletion_job" earliest=-7d
    | stats latest(job_status) as last_status latest(_time) as last_run by dataset_name
]
| eval overdue=if(last_status!="success" OR (now()-last_run)/86400 > scheduled_interval_days, 1, 0)
| where overdue=1
| table dataset_name, retention_days, scheduled_interval_days, last_status, last_run, overdue
description: Storage limitation requires defined retention and deletion. Beyond index retention (UC-22.1.4), this use case tracks automated purge jobs, API deletion endpoints, and backup rotation for personal-data datasets — proving deletion automation runs successfully.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app, backup and sourcetypes deletion_job. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Export dataset-level retention policy to `gdpr_retention_schedule.csv`; (2) instrument deletion jobs with structured logs; (3) alert data owners on failed or missed runs; (4) correlate with object storage lifecycle events; (5) include metrics in DPO quarterly report. Indexes required: `index=app`, `index=backup`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=deletion_job`.
visualization: Table (failed purges), Single value (datasets overdue), Timeline (job runs), Bar chart (failures by system).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Veeam App for Splunk (Splunkbase 7312).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=backup`, `aws:cloudtrail` (DeleteObject lifecycle), application deletion job logs (HEC), `gdpr_retention_schedule.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export dataset-level retention policy to `gdpr_retention_schedule.csv`; (2) instrument deletion jobs with structured logs; (3) alert data owners on failed or missed runs; (4) correlate with object storage lifecycle events; (5) include metrics in DPO quarterly report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_retention_schedule.csv
| join dataset_name [
    search index=app sourcetype="deletion_job" earliest=-7d
    | stats latest(job_status) as last_status latest(_time) as last_run by dataset_name
]
| eval overdue=if(last_status!="success" OR (now()-last_run)/86400 > scheduled_interval_days, 1, 0)
| where overdue=1
| table dataset_name, retention_days, scheduled_interval_days, last_status, last_run, overdue
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Storage Limitation Automation Evidence (Art. 25(2), Art. 5(1)(e))** — Storage limitation requires defined retention and deletion. Beyond index retention (UC-22.1.4), this use case tracks automated purge jobs, API deletion endpoints, and backup rotation for personal-data datasets — proving deletion automation runs successfully.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=backup`, `aws:cloudtrail` (DeleteObject lifecycle), application deletion job logs (HEC), `gdpr_retention_schedule.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Veeam App for Splunk (Splunkbase 7312). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where overdue=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: dataset_name, last_run, last_status, overdue, retention_days, scheduled_interval_days.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed purges), Single value (datasets overdue), Timeline (job runs), Bar chart (failures by system).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for storage limitation requires defined retention and deletion. beyond index retention (.1.4), this use case tracks automated purge jobs, api deletion endpoints, and backup rotation for personal-d. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
